name: Nia Village Midwifery
bio: Nia Village Midwifery provides full-spectrum homebirth care rooted in African birth traditions and evidence-based practice. Serving Atlanta families with love and expertise for over 12 years.
specialties: Water birth, VBAC support, herbal remedies, birth ceremonies

name: Roots & Wings Birth Collective
bio: A Black woman-owned birth center offering holistic, family-centered care in a warm, home-like environment. We honor the wisdom of ancestral birth knowledge alongside modern midwifery practice.
specialties: Birthing suites, sibling-present births, postpartum healing circles, placenta services

name: Sankofa Midwife Care
bio: Sankofa means 'go back and fetch it' — we bring ancestral birth wisdom forward to serve Black families with the dignity and care they deserve. Specializing in high-risk and VBAC homebirths.
specialties: High-risk homebirth, VBAC, doula integration, sliding scale fees available

name: Uhuru Birth Services
bio: Uhuru serves Detroit's Black community with comprehensive maternal and reproductive health care, emphasizing community education, birth justice advocacy, and multigenerational healing.
specialties: Community workshops, birth justice advocacy, teen parent support, group prenatal care

name: Ashé Midwifery & Wellness
bio: Ashé Midwifery offers boutique, relationship-based midwifery care for Washington DC families seeking a personalized, culturally affirming birth experience guided by a board-certified CNM.
specialties: Homebirth, birth center transfers, wellness care, luxury postpartum planning

name: Mama Earth Birth Collective
bio: Mama Earth serves Southern California's Black birthing community with an intersectional lens, honoring every family's journey with compassion, expertise, and deep cultural understanding.
specialties: Waterbirth, placenta services, multilingual support (Spanish/Haitian Creole), LGBTQ+ affirming
